name: Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop
name_original: Camino del Pósito desde el Puente de la Cerrada — circular por la ribera del Río Guadalquivir
description: Jog the moderate 7.3-mile Camino del Pósito circular route from Puente de la Cerrada, enjoying the Río Guadalquivir in Alto Guadalquivir Nat
region_intro: Jogging in Alto Guadalquivir offers routes through diverse natural landscapes, characterized by the Guadalquivir River and its associated wetlands. The region features riverine habitats, gallery forests, and riparian groves, providing varied terrain for running. Extensive olive groves also mark the countryside, reflecting its agricultural heritage. The area includes parts of the Sierra Morena foothills, offering some elevation changes. Best jogging routes in Alto Guadalquivir The most popular jogging route is Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the banks of the Guadalquivir River. Another top favourite among local runners is Laguna de la Herradura Loop, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This route circles the Laguna de la Herradura, offering views of the wetland environment. Local runners also love the Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop from Jódar, a 9.9 miles (16.0 km) trail leading through the foothills of the Sierra Morena, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Jogging in Alto Guadalquivir is defined by riverine paths, wetland areas, and olive groves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Alto Guadalquivir's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alto Guadalquivir?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;10&lt;/strong&gt; dedicated running routes in Alto Guadalquivir, offering a variety of experiences for joggers of all levels. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from riverbanks to olive groves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Alto Guadalquivir?
A: Jogging routes in Alto Guadalquivir feature a mix of terrain. You'll find paths along riverine habitats and wetlands, through gallery forests, and amidst extensive olive groves. Some routes, particularly those closer to the Sierra Morena foothills, may include moderate elevation changes, offering a varied and engaging running experience.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Alto Guadalquivir offers &lt;strong&gt;1 easy route&lt;/strong&gt; perfect for beginners or those seeking a relaxed jog. Additionally, there are &lt;strong&gt;6 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt; that provide a good balance of challenge and accessibility, often following flatter river paths.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Alto Guadalquivir region?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in Alto Guadalquivir are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A notable example is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367475091/posito-trail-and-rio-guadalquivir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pósito Trail and Río Guadalquivir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic 11.7 km run along the river.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Alto Guadalquivir?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see the majestic Guadalquivir River, its associated wetlands, and the unique environment around the dams like Puente de la Cerrada and Doña Aldonza, which includes the resurgent lake of La Herradura. The area is also a Special Protection Area (SPA) for birds, so you might spot various aquatic species like herons and mallards, especially around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367488161/laguna-de-la-herradura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Herradura Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Alto Guadalquivir provides &lt;strong&gt;3 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362857691/serrezuela-de-bedmar-loop-from-jodar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop from Jódar&lt;/a&gt;, a 16.0 km trail that leads through the foothills of the Sierra Morena and involves significant elevation changes.

Q: Is Alto Guadalquivir suitable for family-friendly running or jogging with children?
A: While specific routes are not designated 'family-friendly' in the data, the region's easy and moderate routes, particularly those along flatter river sections or around lakes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367488161/laguna-de-la-herradura-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Herradura Loop&lt;/a&gt;, could be suitable for families depending on the children's fitness levels. These paths often offer pleasant scenery and less strenuous terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Alto Guadalquivir?
A: The diverse landscapes of Alto Guadalquivir make it enjoyable for jogging across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, ideal for longer runs. While summers can be warm, early mornings or late evenings provide cooler conditions. Winters are typically mild, allowing for comfortable running throughout the day.

Q: Are there any long-distance running routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those looking for longer runs, Alto Guadalquivir offers routes that extend over 20 km. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1362863562/serrezuela-de-bedmar-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serrezuela de Bedmar Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a challenging 22.7 km route that takes you through varied terrain, perfect for a longer endurance run.

Q: Are there places to stay or eat near the running trails in Alto Guadalquivir?
A: Yes, the region offers options for accommodation and dining. For those looking to stay close to nature, &lt;strong&gt;Camping La Albolafia&lt;/strong&gt; is an available attraction. You'll also find various cafes, pubs, and accommodations in the towns and villages within the Alto Guadalquivir comarca, such as Montoro and Hornachuelos, which are often near trailheads or accessible after a run.

Q: What makes the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Reserve a unique place for running?
A: The Alto Guadalquivir Natural Reserve is unique due to its vast wetland environment, formed by sections of the Guadalquivir River and its dams. Running here means experiencing lush riverine habitats, gallery forests, and riparian groves. The area is a Special Protection Area for birds, offering a chance to observe diverse aquatic species, enhancing the natural ambiance with the refreshing sound of the wind through the trees.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the olive groves?
A: Yes, the countryside of Alto Guadalquivir is characterized by extensive olive groves, reflecting its agricultural heritage. Many running routes will take you through or alongside these picturesque groves, offering a distinct and scenic backdrop to your run. While not explicitly named, routes traversing the broader landscape will often feature these iconic views.
tips:
A path that allows us to see the entire bank of the Río Guadalquivir, in areas that are more open to vegetation or more closed, depending on the area.
The path goes to the Cortijo del Pósito from the Puente de la Cerrada.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 11.7 kilometers long and takes about 1 hour and 26 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Camino del Pósito?
faqs.answer: The trail is primarily composed of paved surfaces, offering a generally smooth experience suitable for sustained jogging. While mostly paved, some sections might feature well-maintained unpaved paths, providing a varied underfoot experience.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Río Guadalquivir?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy continuous views of the Río Guadalquivir. The landscape shifts from open vistas to denser vegetation, creating a natural tunnel effect in places. The route starts near the natural gorge of Puente de la Cerrada, with views of a reservoir and hydroelectric power station, all within the rich natural environment of the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Park.

faqs.question: Is the Camino del Pósito suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Classified as having 'moderate running difficulty,' this trail is best suited for joggers with a good fitness level. While it has a moderate elevation change of about 90 meters, it's more rolling than steep, offering a good workout without being overly strenuous.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and what are the parking options?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that starts from Puente de la Cerrada. You should find parking available in the vicinity of Puente de la Cerrada, which serves as a convenient access point for the trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Cazorla region generally offers pleasant conditions in spring and autumn, with milder temperatures ideal for jogging. Summer can be very hot, while winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter weather. Always check the local forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Camino del Pósito?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails within the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Park. However, it is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to jog the Camino del Pósito or access the general area of the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Park where it is located.

faqs.question: Are there any specific points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The entire route offers beautiful natural scenery along the Río Guadalquivir. A notable feature is the Pósito Trail itself, which provides continuous river views. The starting point, Puente de la Cerrada, is nestled in a natural gorge and is near a reservoir and hydroelectric power station, offering interesting sights.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route also pass through the Vía pecuaria del Puente de la Cerrada, which is an official livestock trail.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near Puente de la Cerrada?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is immersed in nature, you may find local establishments in the nearby villages or towns surrounding Puente de la Cerrada where you can get refreshments or a meal after your jog.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: As the trail is located within the Alto Guadalquivir Natural Park, a protected wetland, you might spot various bird species, especially those typical of riparian environments. Keep an eye out for local flora and fauna that thrive in this rich ecological zone.
